routineText:
09:00 — Wake up & go to Work
09:00
      — (4h) Work & Water 1L
13:00
13:00 — Black coffee + 1 tsp of sugar

-----------------------
| 14:00 — 🌅 Breakfast
| — whole wheat bread 25g
| — 2 white of eggs boiled
| — 2 yolk of eggs boiled
| — 5g of olive oil
| — mint tea + 1 tsp of sugar
| — cumin & salt
| — oat 35g + water
| — 1 scoop of protein powder
| — 1 avocado 200g whole
-----------------------

14:00 — return to Work
14:00
      — (4h) Work & Water 1L
18:00
18:00 — Out from work

-----------------------
| 18:00 — ☀️ Lunch
| — kefta 90/10 250g
| — khobz complet 25g
| — 300g legume sauté (carrot, courgette, onion)
-----------------------

18:00 — Black Coffee
18:00
      — (4h) Rest or Billard & Water 1L
21:00
21:00 — Back Home

-----------------------
| 21:00 — 🌙 Dinner
| — 1 scoop of protein powder
| — 1 perly nature
| — 2g Almonds
| — 2g Walnuts
| — 2g Cashews
| — 2g Pistachios
| — 1 kiwi
-----------------------

01:00 — Sleep

adjustments.reason: This is the corrected actual breakfast, not an optimization — Mehdi only ate 2 whites + 2 yolks. It costs 10.8g protein and 51 kcal vs the original plan.
Directly replaces the protein lost from the 3 skipped egg whites — closes the protein gap almost exactly (+25g vs -10.8g needed, with margin for the calorie shortfall too).
Adds carbs, fiber and a big vitamin C boost to close the remaining calorie/carb gap — low-FODMAP and gas-safe, no bread or dairy increase needed.

verdict.text_html: Skipped 3 egg whites — 1,561 kcal (−239 vs rest target). Protein 85%, carbs 80% — lunch & dinner as planned won't close the gap.

coverage.name: Calories
coverage.note: 239 kcal under rest target — missing 3 egg whites (−51 kcal) plus lunch/dinner unchanged

coverage.name: Protein
coverage.note: Kefta 50g, whey ×2 50g, 2 egg whites 7.2g, 2 yolks 5.4g, rest from oat/bread/perly — 3 fewer whites cost 10.8g

coverage.name: Carbs
coverage.note: Oat 35g and bread ×2 at minimum keep carbs under — needs a dinner boost

coverage.name: Fat
coverage.note: 2 yolks + avocado + kefta + olive oil land almost exactly on target

coverage.name: Fiber
coverage.note: Avocado 9g, legumes 5.5g, oat 3.7g, khobz ×2 3g, kiwi 2.4g

coverage.name: Gas / bloat risk
coverage.note: Bread twice kept at minimum 25g each — reduced risk

coverage.name: Protocol adherence
coverage.note: Bread-twice intentional at minimum weight — cumin ✓, dinner 21h ✓; protein shortfall from skipped whites

coverage.name: Vitamin A
coverage.note: Carrot 835µgRAE + 2 yolks 130µgRAE — unaffected by fewer whites

coverage.name: Selenium
coverage.note: 2 whites 14µg + 2 yolks 18µg + kefta 50µg — still well covered

coverage.name: Vitamin B12
coverage.note: Kefta 5.5µg + 2 egg yolks 0.6µg — unaffected by fewer whites

good: Selenium and B12 still comfortably covered despite 3 fewer whites
Carrot + 2 yolks → Vitamin A still above target (107%)
bad: Protein at 85% — skipping 3 egg whites costs 10.8g protein and 51 kcal
Calories 239 kcal under rest target — the biggest gap of the day
Carbs at 80% — under target, same root cause as calories
Riboflavin B2 drops to ~89% without the extra whites

fullTable.note: Kefta 50g, whey ×2 50g, 2 whites 7.2g, 2 yolks 5.4g, oat 5.9g, perly 6.5g
3 × 1L blocks from schedule — solid hydration

2 egg yolks only — structural gap without fatty fish

Kefta 5.5µg dominant, 2 egg yolks add 0.6µg

2 egg yolks ~10µg, perly small, iodized salt — structural gap

2 egg whites 14µg + 2 yolks 18µg + kefta 50µg — still well covered

Kefta 11.25mg dominant — close but under target
Avocado 0.26mg, oat 0.13mg
2 yolks 294mg + kefta 163mg + avocado 20mg — unaffected by fewer whites
Fromage frais mild — consider swapping for raib for better gut value
Kefta, khobz ×2 at 25g, cooking salt — lower than usual, 3 fewer whites (−165mg)
Carrot (beta-carotene), kiwi (Vit C), avocado (glutathione), oat
Whey ×2 ~500mg, kefta ~500mg, 2 yolks ~200mg — well above
Oat 0.69mg main source, avocado 0.19mg, carrot 0.14mg
Whole grains (oat, khobz ×2 at 25g) and eggs
2 yolks 2.66mg, avocado 1.9mg, kefta 0.95mg — target covered
2 egg yolks 50µg — still well above target

verdict.text_html: Extra dinner whey scoop + a 2nd kiwi fix it — 1,719 kcal (−81 vs rest target). Protein 100%, carbs 91%, fat 101% — all green.

coverage.name: Calories
coverage.note: Within 81 kcal — extra whey scoop + 2nd kiwi close the gap left by the skipped whites

coverage.name: Protein
coverage.note: Extra dinner whey scoop (+25g) fully offsets the 3 missing egg whites (−10.8g), with margin to spare

coverage.name: Carbs
coverage.note: 2nd kiwi (+11.8g) brings carbs into the good zone without more bread

coverage.name: Fat
coverage.note: Essentially unchanged — still dialled in from breakfast + lunch

coverage.name: Fiber
coverage.note: 2nd kiwi adds 2.4g more fiber, near the top of target range

coverage.name: Gas / bloat risk
coverage.note: Bread twice at 25g each unchanged — extra whey/kiwi are both gas-safe

coverage.name: Protocol adherence
coverage.note: Cumin ✓, dinner 21h ✓, no dairy/bread increase — protein target now met

coverage.name: Vitamin A
coverage.note: Unchanged — carrot + 2 yolks still above target

coverage.name: Selenium
coverage.note: Extra whey scoop nudges it further above target

coverage.name: Vitamin C
coverage.note: 2nd kiwi nearly doubles vitamin C coverage

good: All 3 macros green — protein 100%, carbs 91%, fat 101%, calories within 81 kcal of target
Extra dinner whey scoop fully replaces the protein lost from 3 skipped egg whites
Riboflavin B2 recovers to 101% — the extra scoop closes the gap the missing whites opened
Vitamin C nearly doubles to 199% — 2nd kiwi is a strong antioxidant bonus
No bread, dairy, or raw-veg increase needed — gas risk stays low–moderate
bad: Choline at 87% — 2 yolks vs 3 is the structural driver, unaffected by this fix
Omega-3 minimal — structural gap on fish-free days
3 whey scoops today (breakfast + 2 at dinner) — fine occasionally, not a daily pattern
